title: Caryophyllata reptans
intro: Caryophyllata reptans, also known as Creeping Juniper or Juniperus communis var. saxatilis, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Caryophyllata reptans include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Caryophyllata reptans is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil, often used to treat urinary issues, bronchitis, and skin conditions, as well as to support liver and kidney function.

properties: Caryophyllata reptans, also known as Sweet Woodruff, has been traditionally used for its therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and soothing skin irritations. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antioxidant and antispasmodic effects, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. parts: Caryophyllata reptans, also known as the creeping Jenny or moneywort, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's stems, leaves, and roots are used as medicinal parts in various treatments. The stems and leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. side_effects: Caryophyllata reptans, also known as sticky willow or salsify, has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, but like any plant, it can have side effects. Some reported side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. Individuals with pre-existing conditions, such as diabetes or bleeding disorders, should consult a healthcare professional before using this plant.
